source.title: Aspirin Use to Prevent Cardiovascular Disease

signals: Grade: C and Grade: D recommendations
adults without signs or symptoms of CVD

extraction.summary: The USPSTF recommends shared decision-making for aspirin initiation in adults aged 40-59 years with ≥10% 10-year CVD risk for primary prevention. Aspirin should not be initiated in adults ≥60 years for primary CVD prevention due to increased bleeding risk with age.

population.risk_criteria: For ages 40-59: ≥10% 10-year CVD risk; For ages ≥60: not recommended regardless of CVD risk
population.disease_definition: Primary prevention of cardiovascular disease (CVD) in adults without signs, symptoms, or known CVD
population.exclusions: Adults with signs or symptoms of CVD, known CVD, or increased bleeding risk (history of GI ulcers, recent bleeding, medications increasing bleeding risk)
population.severity_staging: Primary prevention population only - secondary prevention not addressed

key_thresholds.context: Minimum risk level to consider aspirin in ages 40-59
Age at which aspirin initiation is not recommended
Age around which clinicians should consider stopping aspirin

recommendations.recommendation: For adults aged 40-59 years with ≥10% 10-year CVD risk: individualized decision-making to initiate low-dose aspirin
recommendations.notes: Use shared decision-making considering benefits, harms, and patient values/preferences

recommendations.recommendation: For adults 60 years or older: do not initiate aspirin for primary prevention of CVD
recommendations.notes: Increased bleeding risk with age outweighs benefits

medications.indication: Primary prevention of CVD in selected adults aged 40-59 with ≥10% 10-year CVD risk

red_flags: Taking medications that increase bleeding risk
Signs or symptoms of existing CVD
extraction.follow_up: Consider stopping aspirin use around age 75 years; ongoing monitoring for bleeding complications
extraction.escalation_path: If bleeding complications occur, discontinue aspirin and manage bleeding appropriately. Regular reassessment of CVD risk-benefit ratio, especially as patient ages.
